response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and web engineer with an unusually broad career spanning web, mobile, and embedded systems, but the role demands deep modern C++ or Rust expertise applied to geospatial routing data pipelines — an area where his profile is critically weak. His C++ exposure is limited to a bachelor thesis project and brief component work at National Instruments over two decades ago, and Rust does not appear anywhere. His GIS work through GISCollective and Green Map is web-tier (Node.js, Ember.js, DLang) rather than the low-level tile generation and routing pipeline processing required here. The candidate would need a fundamental reorientation of his core skill set to become viable for this position.
strengths: Genuine geospatial and GIS domain exposure through GISCollective and Green Map, demonstrating familiarity with the problem space
Demonstrated ability to build high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Long track record of Linux-based development and infrastructure work across multiple roles
concerns: No meaningful modern C++ or Rust experience — the two required primary languages — with only legacy, incidental C++ use from early career
Zero experience with routing tiles, navigation guidance systems, or production map data pipelines, which are the core deliverables of this role
GIS experience is entirely web-tier (REST APIs, frontend frameworks), not the backend data transformation and tile-generation pipelines the role requires

key_factors.factor: No routing or navigation tile pipeline experience
